title: Electrician
description: An electrician in India installs, maintains, and repairs electrical systems in residential, commercial, and industrial settings. They work with various electrical equipment, including wiring, circuits, and electrical panels, ensuring safe and efficient electrical supply. Electricians often work in teams, following safety protocols and adhering to local electrical codes and regulations.
top_govt_colleges: National Institute of Electronics and Information Technology (NIELIT)
Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) - Electrical Engineering
National Institute of Technology (NIT) - Electrical Engineering
top_private_colleges: Sagar Institute of Research and Technology (SIRT)
Shri Ram College of Engineering and Management (SRCEM)
Jagran Institute of Management and Technology (JIMT)

career_progression.year_0_2: Assist experienced electricians, learn electrical codes and regulations, and develop basic electrical skills.
career_progression.year_3_5: Work independently on small projects, develop troubleshooting skills, and take on more responsibility.
career_progression.year_6_10: Lead teams, design and implement electrical systems, and oversee project execution.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Start an electrical contracting business, offer electrical services to residential and commercial clients.
career_progression.global_pathway: Work in international companies, participate in electrical projects abroad, or pursue higher education in electrical engineering.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting salary is low, but opportunities for growth and advancement are available.
common_misconceptions: Electricians do not need formal education.
Electricians work only in construction sites.
